Title: Identification of triacylglycerol remodeling mechanism to synthesize unusual fatty acid containing oils

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 2S albumin promoter (seed-specific RNAi in Physaria); beta-conglycinin promoter (seed-specific overexpression in Arabidopsis); GAL1 promoter (yeast expression)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during developing seeds; more highly expressed than other candidate lipase genes during seed development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds; transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for localization
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TAG lipase-like protein; putative lipase with hydrophobic N-terminus/transmembrane domain
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; puncta surrounding lipid droplets/oil bodies, likely ER–lipid droplet junction
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Hydroxy fatty acid-containing triacylglycerol, especially 2HFA-TAG enriched in lesquerolic acid
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant storage oil; unusual fatty acid feedstock with nutritional relevance of plant oils and industrial uses of hydroxy fatty acids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast expression and lipid profiling; purified enzyme in vitro lipase assay; competitive lipase assay; GFP localization; ER co-localization; split-ubiquitin Y2H; BiFC; seed-specific RNAi knockdown in Physaria; transgenic overexpression in HFA-producing Arabidopsis; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of HFA-containing TAG by remodeling TAG rather than degrading seed oil overall; RNAi reduces seed oil and HFA content, while expression in RcFAH Arabidopsis increases total oil and HFA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development; HFA-producing RcFAH genetic background; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana high-oil leaves; galactose induction in yeast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PfeTAGL1 is a functional TAG lipase experimentally validated in yeast and with purified protein in vitro. 2. It preferentially removes common fatty acids over hydroxy fatty acids from TAG, consistent with generating sn-2,3-DAG during TAG remodeling. 3. PfeTAGL1 localizes to the ER and puncta around oil bodies and physically interacts with PfeDGAT1, supporting a TAG-remodeling metabolon. 4. Knockdown in Physaria reduces total oil and HFA accumulation. 5. Expression in RcFAH Arabidopsis unexpectedly increases total oil and HFA accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering TAG remodeling to increase unusual fatty acid accumulation in crop seed oils; can be used alone or with DGATs to enhance HFA deposition and alter oil composition
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi reduces total seed oil; expression increases PUFA in TAG fractions, indicating broader flux redistribution; effects may depend on availability of suitable DAG substrates and acyltransferases
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike PfeSDP1, PfeTAGL1 did not behave as a net TAG turnover lipase in engineering context; likely acts in biosynthetic remodeling near ER–lipid droplet junction
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed transcriptomics referenced from prior studies; no accession reported in provided text

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial acylation of PC-derived DAG in TAG remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream / terminal TAG-synthesis step; first acyltransferase step of TAG remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: sn-1,2-DAG, preferentially non-HFA-containing DAG; acyl-CoA including 20:1OH-CoA and 18:1-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to sn-1,2-DAG to form TAG; likely adds HFA to the sn-3 position of PC-derived DAG during initial TAG remodeling step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in first TAG remodeling acylation; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria fendleri; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Physaria_fen32; H1246 yeast; RcFAH background Arabidopsis line
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 promoter in yeast; 2S albumin promoter in Arabidopsis; 2S albumin promoter for RNAi in Physaria
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during seed development, 2–10 fold higher than PfeDGAT2/3 or PfePDAT1 across seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds; transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana for localization/BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Hydroxy fatty acid-containing TAG, especially 2HFA-TAG via precursor 1HFA-TAG
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant storage oil; hydroxy fatty acid feedstock with nutritional and industrial value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; microsomal in vitro DGAT assays; DAG stereoselectivity assays; GFP localization; Y2H; BiFC; seed-specific RNAi knockdown in Physaria; transgenic expression in RcFAH Arabidopsis; seed oil/metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TAG and HFA accumulation; functions mainly in the initial synthesis of 1HFA-TAG from sn-1,2-DAG
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development; RcFAH HFA-producing Arabidopsis background; yeast HFA feeding and microsomal assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PfeDGAT1 restores TAG synthesis in TAG-deficient yeast. 2. It prefers sn-1,2-DAG and common-fatty-acid-containing DAG over HFA-DAG. 3. It likely catalyzes the first acyltransferase step of TAG remodeling, producing initial 1HFA-TAG. 4. It localizes to the ER and interacts with PfeTAGL1. 5. RNAi knockdown lowers total oil and HFA content, while transgenic expression in RcFAH Arabidopsis increases oil and HFA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering increased seed oil and HFA accumulation, especially in hosts where sn-1,2-DAG predominates; suitable for stacking with TAGL1 and DGAT2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate preference may limit effectiveness in systems lacking appropriate sn-1,2-DAG/HFA substrates; expression shifts TAG species toward more oleic acid and densipolic acid patterns
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: More effective than PfeDGAT2 in increasing total oil/HFA in Arabidopsis despite lower in vitro preference for HFA-DAG, likely due to substrate availability in host
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed transcriptomics referenced from prior studies; no accession reported in provided text

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Second acylation step using sn-2,3-DAG in TAG remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream / terminal TAG-synthesis step; second acyltransferase step of TAG remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: sn-2,3-DAG and sn-1,2-DAG; HFA-containing DAG; acyl-CoA including 20:1OH-CoA with preference for HFA-containing substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to DAG, with preference for HFA-containing and sn-2,3-DAG substrates, producing final 2HFA-TAG
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in final acylation toward 2HFA-TAG; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria fendleri; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Physaria_fen32; H1246 yeast; RcFAH background Arabidopsis line
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 promoter in yeast; 2S albumin promoter in Arabidopsis; 2S albumin promoter for RNAi in Physaria
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during developing seeds but lower than PfeDGAT1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds; transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana for localization/BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Hydroxy fatty acid-containing TAG, especially 2HFA-TAG enriched in lesquerolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant storage oil; hydroxy fatty acid feedstock with nutritional and industrial value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; microsomal in vitro DGAT assays; DAG stereoselectivity assays; GFP localization; Y2H/BiFC negative interaction tests; seed-specific RNAi knockdown in Physaria; transgenic expression in RcFAH Arabidopsis; seed oil/metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes final accumulation of 2HFA-TAG by selectively utilizing HFA-containing and sn-2,3-DAG substrates; RNAi reduces seed oil and HFA content
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development; RcFAH HFA-producing Arabidopsis background; yeast HFA feeding and microsomal assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PfeDGAT2 restores TAG synthesis in yeast, though host context underestimates its HFA-related activity. 2. In vitro it is more active than PfeDGAT1 with HFA-containing DAG and can utilize both sn-1,2- and sn-2,3-DAG, preferring sn-2,3-DAG. 3. It likely catalyzes the second acyltransferase step of TAG remodeling to form final 2HFA-TAG. 4. RNAi knockdown decreases total oil and HFA content in Physaria. 5. Transgenic expression in RcFAH Arabidopsis increases oil and HFA and enriches 20:1/20:1OH in TAG species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target for direct formation of unusual-fatty-acid-rich TAG, especially when combined with a lipase such as PfeTAGL1 to generate preferred sn-2,3-DAG substrates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Less effective than PfeDGAT1 in heterologous systems dominated by sn-1,2-DAG; utility may depend strongly on substrate pool engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No detectable physical interaction with tested lipases or DGATs in Y2H/BiFC in this study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed transcriptomics referenced from prior studies; no accession reported in provided text
